content:
🔥 ShizuWall | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide users with granular control over application network access on Android devices without requiring root privileges or a VPN connection, enhancing privacy and reducing data usage.
✨ Key Features: • No root or VPN required • Per-app system networking control • Privacy-first design • Leverages Shizuku or local ADB daemon
📖 Summary: ShizuWall is a lightweight Android firewall application that allows users to block network connections for specific apps without needing root access or a VPN. It utilizes the Shizuku service or a local ADB daemon to manage network permissions on a per-app basis, offering a privacy-focused solution.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
